title: Fanout Pub/Sub and Subscriber Responsibilities · Runtime Contract: Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must prove one queue per responsibility, late-subscriber behavior, cancellation, and independent consumer ownership; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the subscriber queue per responsibility increment in a Go subscriber responsibility and queue lifecycle map. L…
objectives: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Acknowledge only after a durable effect or safely repeatable handoff and retain message identity, attempt, result, and failure evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility · Theory Subscriber Queue Per Responsib…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 2272 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove one queue per subscriber responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility contract for the supplied data.
